summary:
Nitrous oxide can cause respiratory depression at high concentrations. It also has a potential for abuse due to its psychoactive effects when used recreationally or improperly administered during surgery.
The summary captures two critical issues related to using nitrous oxide as an anesthesia: risk of respiratory suppression from excessive use and misuse leading to psychological side-effects outside medical settings. These are core concerns highlighted by recent studies supporting my previous conclusion while adding nuance about both safety limits and patient behavior risks under improper conditions. The concise presentation maintains focus without extraneous details not directly pertinent to addressing problems specifically tied into administration practices within healthcare contexts involving this particular form of general anesthesia delivery method utilized globally across various surgical procedures today.

The summary captures two critical issues related to using nitrous oxide as an anesthesia: risk of respiratory suppression from excessive use and misuse leading to psychological side-effects outside medical settings. These are core concerns highlighted by recent studies supporting my previous conclusion while adding nuance about both safety limits and patient behavior risks under improper conditions. The concise presentation maintains focus without extraneous details not directly pertinent to addressing problems specifically tied into administration practices within healthcare contexts involving this particular form of general anesthesia delivery method utilized globally across various surgical procedures today.
